name: Product Frontend Backlog Intake Gate
audit_sources: docs/TODO.md Phase 18 completed frontend workflow entries

audit_summary.home_search_filter_navigation: Phase 18.1 already completed shell refresh, view modes, command palette MVP, and filter strip. Current Home flow preserves local view mode, route-aware filters, manual/auto load-more, reading/editor modals, and custom sort DnD.
audit_summary.reading_editor_workflow: Phase 18.2 already completed reading view, editor modal/layout refinement, Preview Editing regression lock, and attachment/image UX pass. Existing hooks remain the intended extension point.
audit_summary.prompt_builder_settings: Phase 18.3 already completed Prompt Builder density pass and Settings tab re-layout. Current pages keep existing contracts and tabs without new backend APIs.
audit_summary.not_reopened: Do not reopen Phase 18 as a broad redesign. Promote only a single small follow-up with a clear browser flow.

candidate_matrix.finding: The Header command palette button currently dispatches a synthetic Ctrl+K KeyboardEvent to the window. This works as a shortcut proxy but makes the palette open state indirectly owned by keyboard handling instead of an explicit UI action.
candidate_matrix.why_small: A narrow frontend-only change can move palette open/toggle ownership into explicit state or a direct callback without changing routes, APIs, storage, schema, notes data, or command behavior.

candidate_matrix.finding: Settings tabs are local UI state only. Deep links could improve repeat navigation but would introduce URL-state behavior that is broader than the smallest current follow-up.
Prompt Builder already has density/layout work. Further action-bar polish should follow visual/browser evidence rather than be selected by default.
Home empty state could offer context-aware actions, but it touches content copy and workflow choices beyond the immediate entrypoint reliability issue.

selected_candidate.title: Command Palette Entrypoint Reliability
selected_candidate.scope: Frontend-only implementation to replace the Header synthetic Ctrl+K event dispatch with an explicit command palette open/toggle path while preserving the existing Ctrl+K keyboard shortcut.
success_criteria: Header command palette button opens the palette without dispatching a synthetic KeyboardEvent.
Ctrl+K / Cmd+K still toggles the palette.
Palette search, Escape close, navigation commands, recent note open, new note action, and theme toggle behavior remain unchanged.
No backend API, DB schema, frontend default API target, Pi/Caddy/service, Go runtime, public exposure, AI/ML, collections, or server-side preference persistence changes.
verification_plan: Browser flow: click Header command palette button, type a query, run Settings navigation, reopen with Ctrl+K/Cmd+K, close with Escape, run New Note action, verify no console errors.
selected_candidate.rollback_plan: Revert only the explicit palette open/toggle state changes and restore the prior keyboard listener behavior if browser flow or typecheck fails.
stop_conditions: The fix requires a new dependency.
The fix requires backend API, schema, route, persistence, or runtime changes.
The browser flow loses Ctrl+K/Cmd+K behavior or existing command actions.
The change expands into broader navigation, Settings, Prompt Builder, or redesign work.

not_authorized_by_22_0: New backend API or DB schema
Pi deploy or live service reload
AI/ML, collaboration, realtime, plugin platform, collections schema, or server-side UI preference persistence
verification_required_for_22_0: tests/test_phase22_product_frontend_backlog_intake.py locks the audit, selected candidate, and forbidden implementation scope
tests/test_phase21_post_push_product_frontend_selection.py continues to lock the Phase 21 branch decision

allowed_next_step.title: Command Palette Entrypoint Reliability
allowed_next_step.scope: Implement only the selected frontend-only command palette entrypoint reliability fix, then verify with typecheck, build, pytest, and browser flow.
not_authorized_without_approval: New backend API or DB schema
Pi deploy or live service reload
